title: Collision Events, Single Resolution, Fragments, Damage, and Lives · Language Clinic: Damage Window
summary: One projectile damages the same rock twice, both loops delete the same sprite, fragments recursively collide during creation, ship damage repeats every frame, and feedback arrives after state removal. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the damage window increment in ordered single-resolution collision response. Learners progress through predi…
objectives: Model lives, invulnerability, respawn safety, visible and nonvisual feedback, and pause behavior using simulation time.
Spawn child sizes, positions, velocities, immunity, and counts after parent resolution under entity budgets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-collision-response-damage-3
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-collision-response-damage-3.
# Case 5798: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def worked_asteroids_collision_response_damage_3(collisions: int = 3, resolved_once: int = 3, invulnerability_ms: int = 1200, duplicate_damage: int = 0, fragments_spawned: int = 6) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "playerworked3",
        "seed5798",
        "input3dekrow",
        "frameworked35798",
        "state5798worked3",
        "playtest3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if collisions < 0 or resolved_once < 0:
        return False, collisions, "response-domain"
    if resolved_once != collisions:
        return False, resolved_once, "response-count"
    if invulnerability_ms < 0 or duplicate_damage > 0:
        return False, duplicate_damage, "damage-window"
    if fragments_spawned < collisions:
        return False, fragments_spawned, "fragment-policy"
    assert resolved_once == collisions
    return True, fragments_spawned, "single-response"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Damage Window evidence · Theory Damage Window
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-damage-window-12ki" that uses case 6294 to resolve each collision once with explicit destruction, fragments, lives, invulnerability, feedback, and event ordering. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Damage Window evidence · Theory Damage Window.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Damage Window into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Damage Window evidence · Theory Damage Window, keep Damage Window connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-damage-window-12ki
# Competency: asteroids-damage-window.
# Case 6294: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def evidence_asteroids_damage_window_12ki(collisions: int = 3, resolved_once: int = 3, invulnerability_ms: int = 1200, duplicate_damage: int = 0, fragments_spawned: int = 6) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_12ki = "12ki-ik21-6294"
    assert evidence_variant_12ki
    evidence_axes_12ki = (
        "player12ki",
        "seed6294",
        "inputik21",
        "frame12ki6294",
        "state629412ki",
        "playtestik216294",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_12ki)) == 6
    if collisions < 0 or resolved_once < 0:
        return False, collisions, "response-domain"
    if resolved_once != collisions:
        return False, resolved_once, "response-count"
    if invulnerability_ms < 0 or duplicate_damage > 0:
        return False, duplicate_damage, "damage-window"
    if fragments_spawned < collisions:
        return False, fragments_spawned, "fragment-policy"
    assert resolved_once == collisions
    return True, fragments_spawned, "single-response"
